title: Automation-first design contract test (HBR-QUIET-002 design audit)
goal: Land the automation-first design contract test that audits every Tauri command + UI surface introduced by KERNEL-004 and proves it is reachable without OS-level keyboard injection, cursor movement, focus stealing, or foregrounding. Negative: any path requiring those mechanisms fails the gate.
expected_diff_shape: New test file src/backend/handshake_core/tests/automation_first_audit_tests.rs that walks the Tauri command inventory and asserts each is callable via IPC alone; CI artifact captures the audit report.
unblock_conditions: All A.4 foundation MTs merged so the audit has the full quiet-mode + focus-audit + injection-test + lint + foreground-exception stack to assert against.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.6 + HBR-QUIET-002 evidence_kind=automation_path_audit. The audit walks the Tauri command inventory (extracted via a build-time scanner that greps for `#[tauri::command]`) and for each command runs: (1) call via IPC mock — must succeed without any OS-level input; (2) call via IPC while focus-audit (MT-015) is active — assert zero handshake-owned foreground transitions; (3) call via raw OS keyboard injection (MT-016) — assert zero invocation count. Commands that legitimately require foreground are exempted only if they appear in a `foreground_exempt_commands` allowlist documented inline (currently empty; if a future command needs exemption it must declare via MT-019 ForegroundException). The Node helper script `automation-first-audit.mjs` produces a markdown summary report (on-demand only per CX-908) for operator review. Per HBR-QUIET-002 evidence_kind = automation_path_audit this MT is THE wire test that produces the audit evidence. Tests: assert all current KERNEL-004 Tauri commands (from MT-013, MT-018, MT-027, MT-030, MT-036) pass the audit; assert that a synthetic violation (a test command that calls SetForegroundWindow) fails the audit loudly.
risk_if_missed: Without the audit, HBR-QUIET-002 has no end-to-end evidence; individual MTs prove pieces but not the whole-app guarantee.
validator_focus: Audit walks the WHOLE Tauri command inventory (not a hand-picked subset); synthetic violation fixture proves the audit can catch a regression.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Audit walks the FULL Tauri command inventory (auto-discovered, not hand-listed).
Synthetic-violation fixture confirms the audit can catch regressions.
foreground_exempt_commands allowlist is documented and initially empty.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: automation-first-audit.mjs walks the full Tauri command inventory (regex over #[tauri::command] in app/src-tauri/src) and detects forbidden OS-foreground/input APIs (SetForegroundWindow, AllowSetForegroundWindow, LockSetForegroundWindow, SendInput, .set_focus, .unminimize, WebviewWindowBuilder::new); on synthetic fixture it correctly emits FORBIDDEN_OS_FOREGROUND_API violations. However the audit is STATIC SOURCE ANALYSIS only - it never actually calls each Tauri command via IPC nor pairs the call with a live focus-audit/keyboard-injection probe as the MT-020 contract demands.
hbr_unsatisfied: HBR-QUIET-003 (process-lifecycle wiring is in MT-015, but MT-020 does not exercise it during the audit run)

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Automation-first design audit does not exist; the whole-app HBR-QUIET-002 wire test is absent and could not function anyway because every dependency MT (014..019) is also unimplemented.
